practice.name: Problem 6 (2025 AMC 8)
practice.statement:
Sekou writes the numbers $15, 16, 17, 18, 19.$ After he erases one of the numbers, the sum of the remaining four numbers is a multiple of $4.$ Which number did he erase?

$\textbf{(A)}\ 15 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 16 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 17 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 18 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 19$

practice.name: Problem 1 (2024 AMC 8)
practice.statement:
What is the ones digit of $$222{,}222 - 22{,}222 - 2{,}222 - 222 - 22 - 2?$$

$\textbf{(A)}\ 0 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 2 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 4 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 6 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 8$

practice.name: Problem 4 (2024 AMC 8)
practice.statement:
When Yunji added all the integers from $1$ to $9,$ she mistakenly left out a number. Her incorrect sum turned out to be a square number. Which number did Yunji leave out?

$\textbf{(A)}\ 5 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 6 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 7 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 8 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 9$

practice.name: Problem 5 (2024 AMC 8)
practice.statement:
Aaliyah rolls two standard $6$-sided dice. She notices that the product of the two numbers rolled is a multiple of $6.$ Which of the following integers cannot be the sum of the two numbers?

$\textbf{(A)}\ 5 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 6 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 7 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 8 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 9$

practice.name: Problem 7 (2018 AMC 8)
practice.statement:
The $5$-digit number $\underline{2}\,\underline{0}\,\underline{1}\,\underline{8}\,\underline{U}$ is divisible by $9.$ What is the remainder when this number is divided by $8$?

$\textbf{(A)}\ 1 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 3 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 5 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 6 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 7$

practice.name: Problem 21 (2017 AMC 8)
practice.statement:
Suppose $a$, $b$, and $c$ are nonzero real numbers, and $a + b + c = 0$. What are the possible value(s) for $\frac{a}{|a|} + \frac{b}{|b|} + \frac{c}{|c|} + \frac{abc}{|abc|}$?

$\textbf{(A)}\ 0 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 1 \text{ and } -1 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 2 \text{ and } -2 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 0, 2, \text{ and } -2 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 0, 1, \text{ and } -1$

practice.name: Problem 4 (2002 AMC 10A)
practice.statement:
For how many positive integers $m$ does there exist at least one positive integer $n$ such that $mn \le m + n$?

$\textbf{(A)}\ 4 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 6 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 9 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 12 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ \text{infinitely many}$
